name: Form

values.name: defaultOptions
values.comment: Default element options.

values.name: errorString
values.comment:
Convert an error value to a string.

This function can be used as a template for your own error messages.

Error value is documented in the [`Form.Error`](Form-Error) module.

values.name: getErrors
values.comment:
Get all form errors as a list.

Empty list is returned if the form contains no errors.

values.name: getRawValue
values.comment:
Get the current form value.

The returned value reflects the current form contents.
It is not normalized, and may not be conforming to the JSON Schema.
To get a normalized value conforming to the JSON Schema, use [`getSubmitValue`](#getSubmitValue).

values.name: getSchema
values.comment: Get the current Schema

values.name: getSubmitValue
values.comment:
Get the current form value.

The value is present only if form validation passes with no errors.

values.name: getUiSchema
values.comment: Get the current UI Schema

values.name: init
values.comment:
Initialize form state.

Supplying anything other than [`defaultOptions`](#defaultOptions) into the `init` function
causes the resulting form to differ from json-forms.io specification. These differences
should be documented.
values.type: UiSchema.DefOptions -> String.String -> Json.Schema.Definitions.Schema -> Maybe.Maybe UiSchema.Internal.UiSchema -> Form.Form

values.name: setSchema
values.comment:
Swap the Schema of an existing form.

Form data is reset. UI Schema is re-generated if it was auto-generated in the first place.

values.name: setUiSchema
values.comment:
Swap the UI Schema of an existing form.

Form data is preserved.

values.name: update
values.comment: Update the form

values.name: validateAllFieldsMsg
values.comment:
Enable form validations for all fields.

Until this message is triggered, fields are validated only after input.

values.name: viewWidget
values.comment:
View a widget.

This function can be used as a template for your own view function.

Widget type is documented in the [`Form.Widget`](Form-Widget) module.

values.name: widget
values.comment:
Render the form into an abstract view representation.

This representation can in turn be rendered into HTML by [`viewWidget`](#viewWidget),
or by a custom function.

Widget type is documented in the [`Form.Widget`](Form-Widget) module.

name: Form.Error
comment:
Form field error handling

@docs Errors, ErrorValue, TextFormat

unions.name: ErrorValue
unions.comment: A single error value for a single field

aliases.name: Errors
aliases.comment: List of all errors in a form. One field may generate multiple entries.

name: Form.Widget
comment:
Abstract form view representation.

This representation is meant to be rendered into HTML. For inspiration, see the
[`Form.Widget.View`](https://github.com/scrive/elm-json-forms/blob/main/src/Form/Widget/View.elm) module.

@docs Widget, Group, Categorization, CategoryButton, Label, Options, Validation, isInvalid, isValid, Control, TextInput, FieldFormat, FieldType, TextArea, Select, RadioGroup, Checkbox, Slider

unions.name: Control
unions.comment: Specific kind of a control element with associated options.

unions.name: FieldType
unions.comment: Type of a text-like input field

unions.name: Validation
unions.comment:
Validation state of a field.

To avoid showing many validation errors for a freshly-displayed form,
the validation state is `NotValidated` until the field is blurred, or
until the `validateAllFieldsMsg` message is sent.

unions.name: Widget
unions.comment:
Root of the form representation.

Widgets can be nested to create complex forms.

aliases.name: Categorization
aliases.comment:
Categorization element.

A categorization element consists of a list of category buttons,
and a list of elements from the chosen category.

`elements` list contains the active category elements,
which should be rendered vertically below category buttons.
aliases.type: { buttons : List.List Form.Widget.CategoryButton, elements : List.List Form.Widget.Widget }

aliases.name: CategoryButton
aliases.comment:
Category button.

A category button is a button that can be clicked to select the active category.

Active category is marked with the `focus` attribute.
aliases.type: { label : String.String, focus : Basics.Bool, onClick : Form.State.Msg }

aliases.name: Checkbox
aliases.comment:
Checkbox control

  - `toggle` - Whether the checkbox should be rendered as a toggle button.
aliases.type: { value : Basics.Bool, onCheck : Basics.Bool -> Form.State.Msg, toggle : Basics.Bool }

aliases.name: Group
aliases.comment:
Labeled group of elements.

Elements in a group are rendered vertically, below the group label.
aliases.type: { label : Maybe.Maybe String.String, elements : List.List Form.Widget.Widget }

aliases.name: Options
aliases.comment:
Control options

Controls should be rendered in accordance with these options.

  - `label` - Label of the control, to be shown above the control.
  - `hideLabel` - Whether the label should be hidden.
    If `True`, the `aria-label` attribute should be filled with the `label` value.
  - `id` - Unique identifier for the control, to be used as the "id" attribute.
  - `required` - Whether the control should be marked as required, with an asterisk or similar.
    Note that the control may or may not _actually_ be required.
  - `validation` - Validation state of the control, possibly containing an error value.
  - `description` - Description of the control, to be shown below the control.
  - `onFocus` - Used only to show field descriptions on focus.
    If this feature is not used, the `onFocus` message does not need to be triggered.
  - `onBlur` - Used to show field validation for the first time.
  - `trim` - Whether the control should be short
aliases.type: { label : String.String, hideLabel : Basics.Bool, id : String.String, disabled : Basics.Bool, required : Basics.Bool, validation : Form.Widget.Validation, description : Maybe.Maybe String.String, onFocus : Form.State.Msg, onBlur : Form.State.Msg, trim : Basics.Bool }

aliases.name: RadioGroup
aliases.type: { valueList : List.List { id : String.String, label : String.String, checked : Basics.Bool, onClick : Form.State.Msg }, vertical : Basics.Bool }

aliases.name: Select
aliases.type: { valueList : List.List { label : String.String, selected : Basics.Bool }, onChange : String.String -> Form.State.Msg }

aliases.name: Slider
aliases.comment:
Slider control.

This element is implemented for completeness, but there are probably not many use-cases for it.
aliases.type: { value : String.String, min : String.String, max : String.String, step : String.String, onInput : String.String -> Form.State.Msg }

aliases.name: TextArea
aliases.comment:
Text area control

`maxLength` should inform the "maxlength" attribute of the input field.
aliases.type: { value : String.String, maxLength : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, onInput : String.String -> Form.State.Msg }

aliases.name: TextInput
aliases.comment:
Text input control

`maxLength` should inform the "maxlength" attribute of the input field.
aliases.type: { value : String.String, fieldType : Form.Widget.FieldType, maxLength : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, onInput : String.String -> Form.State.Msg }

values.name: isInvalid
values.comment:
Convenience function to check if a validation state is invalid.

If so, the associated error value should be rendered.

values.name: isValid
values.comment: Convenience function to check if a validation state is valid.

name: Json.Pointer
comment:
This module implements JSON Pointer as per [RFC 6901](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6901).

@docs Pointer, decode, fromString, toString, pointedValue

aliases.name: Pointer
aliases.comment:
Pointer path, represented as `List String`.

Pointers have a standardized text format. For example:

    "#/customers/0/name"

values.name: decode
values.comment: Decode a pointer

values.name: fromString
values.comment: Construct a pointer from a `String`.

values.name: pointedValue
values.comment:
Get a sub-value which is pointed at by a pointer.

If the pointer does not point to an existing value, `Nothing` is returned.

values.name: toString
values.comment: Convert a pointer to a `String`.

name: UiSchema

values.name: decode
values.comment: UiSchema Decoder

values.name: defaultOptions
values.comment: Default options for the UI Schema

values.name: fromString
values.comment: Validate and produce UI Schema from JSON String

values.name: generate
values.comment:
Generate a UI Schema from a Schema.

The generated UI schema contains vertical layout with controls for all primitive types.
